route.name: Great Divide Mountain Bike Route (GDMBR)

sources.title: BIKEPACKING.com - Great Divide Mountain Bike Route (GDMBR)

sources.title: RideWithGPS - Great Divide Mountain Bike Route region

route.sourceNote: Planning package geometry resolved from routes/great-divide-mountain-bike-route-gdmbr.gpx. Direct public BIKEPACKING.com Tour Divide 2026 GPX with POIs; one full track, no artificial routing.
route.description: Full Great Divide Mountain Bike Route / Tour Divide corridor from Banff, Alberta to Antelope Wells, New Mexico using the public BIKEPACKING.com GPX with POIs.
route.surfaceMix: Mostly dirt/gravel roads with limited singletrack; endurance, remoteness, weather, and logistics are the dominant difficulty.
route.bestSeason: Commonly a summer route, but snowpack, high passes, monsoon storms, wildfire/smoke, heat, and New Mexico desert exposure are major gates.

resources.water: Known long, remote dry stretches require detailed water planning; GPX POIs need current verification.
resources.food: Route towns and services exist but hours, seasonal closures, and longest food carries need current review.
resources.camping: Requires a multi-week camping/lodging strategy across Alberta, Montana, Idaho, Wyoming, Colorado, and New Mexico.
resources.toiletsShowers: Confirm by campground/service source before riding.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest bike shops before promotion to ride-ready.
resources.longestGap: Measure from resolved geometry before ride-ready promotion.

tips: Keep planning_grade until the full GDMBR is broken into a current itinerary with snow/pass checks, fire/smoke/forest closure review, water and food carries, camping/lodging plan, border logistics, and section reroutes verified.
hazards: Current snowpack/passability and seasonal timing checks required.
Fire, smoke, forest closure, and reroute checks required across the full corridor.
Exact multi-week camping/lodging, food, water, and bear-country plan unresolved.
Longest carry and remote bailout audit required before ride-ready use.
